$comment: Data-driven expected roster for scripts/check-runner-health.mjs (Story #258). Each entry is one repo whose self-hosted runner fleet the scheduled runner-fleet-health.yml workflow monitors. Adding/removing a runner needs only an edit here — the checker reads GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runners and compares live status against `expectedCount` + `labels`. All nine runners are co-resident on one operator Mac (2026-07-03 runner audit, repo-ops matrix §1a); if that host sleeps, reboots, fills its disk, or a launchd service dies, every listed repo's CI silently queues with no alert until this monitor catches it.
$comment_staleQueuedMinutes: A queued/waiting workflow run older than this many minutes with no online runner matching its labels is flagged as a queue-staleness signal (optional per-repo override via `staleQueuedMinutes`).
